title: LSP4IJ Perl LSP server settings JSON schema
description: JSON schema for perlsp server settings.

perl-lsp.serverPath.description: Absolute path to the perllsp binary. Leave empty to auto-download a release build.
perl-lsp.serverPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the `perllsp` binary. Leave empty to auto-download a release build.

perl-lsp.autoDownload.description: Automatically download the perllsp binary if it is not found locally. Disable this for internal deployments that set serverPath.
perl-lsp.autoDownload.markdownDescription: Automatically download the `perllsp` binary if it is not found locally. Disable this for internal deployments that set `serverPath`.

perl-lsp.includePaths.description: Additional library paths (like use lib) to search for Perl modules. Set this if you see "Can't locate Foo/Bar.pm" style module lookup errors. Relative paths resolve from the workspace root.
perl-lsp.includePaths.markdownDescription: Additional library paths (like `use lib`) to search for Perl modules. Set this if you see `Can't locate Foo/Bar.pm` style module lookup errors. Relative paths resolve from the workspace root.

perl-lsp.enableDiagnostics.description: Enable real-time syntax diagnostics. When disabled, the server will not publish diagnostic errors and warnings.
perl-lsp.enableDiagnostics.markdownDescription: Enable real-time syntax diagnostics. When disabled, the server will not publish diagnostic errors and warnings.

perl-lsp.enableSemanticTokens.description: Enable semantic syntax highlighting. When disabled, the editor falls back to TextMate grammar coloring only.
perl-lsp.enableSemanticTokens.markdownDescription: Enable semantic syntax highlighting. When disabled, the editor falls back to TextMate grammar coloring only.

perl-lsp.enableFormatting.description: Enable document formatting using perltidy. This requires perltidy to be installed on your system.
perl-lsp.enableFormatting.markdownDescription: Enable document formatting using `perltidy`. This requires `perltidy` to be installed on your system.

perl-lsp.formatOnSave.description: Format the document automatically on save. This requires perltidy to be installed.
perl-lsp.formatOnSave.markdownDescription: Format the document automatically on save. This requires `perltidy` to be installed.

perl-lsp.perltidyConfig.description: Path to your .perltidyrc configuration file. If omitted, perl-lsp searches the workspace and home directory.
perl-lsp.perltidyConfig.markdownDescription: Path to your `.perltidyrc` configuration file. If omitted, perl-lsp searches the workspace and home directory.

perl-lsp.perlcritic.enabled.description: Enable external Perl::Critic diagnostics from the VS Code extension.
perl-lsp.perlcritic.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable external `perlcritic` diagnostics from the VS Code extension.

enumDescriptions: Severity 1 - very permissive
Severity 2 - permissive
Severity 3 - balanced default
Severity 4 - strict
Severity 5 - very strict
perl-lsp.perlcritic.severity.description: Perl::Critic minimum severity (1 = least severe/reports more, 5 = most severe/reports less).
perl-lsp.perlcritic.severity.markdownDescription: Perl::Critic minimum severity (`1` = least severe/reports more, `5` = most severe/reports less).

perl-lsp.perlcritic.profile.description: Optional path to a .perlcriticrc profile file used by the extension when synchronizing critic settings.
perl-lsp.perlcritic.profile.markdownDescription: Optional path to a `.perlcriticrc` profile file used by the extension when synchronizing critic settings.

perl-lsp.perlcritic.theme.description: Optional display theme label for PerlCritic settings. Reserved for editor-facing UI organization.
perl-lsp.perlcritic.theme.markdownDescription: Optional display theme label for PerlCritic settings. Reserved for editor-facing UI organization.

perl-lsp.enableRefactoring.description: Enable refactoring-related code actions (extract variable, extract method) when supported by the connected perllsp server.
perl-lsp.enableRefactoring.markdownDescription: Enable refactoring-related code actions (extract variable, extract method) when supported by the connected `perllsp` server.

perl-lsp.enableTestIntegration.description: Enable Test::More and Test2 integration for the VS Code Test Explorer panel.
perl-lsp.enableTestIntegration.markdownDescription: Enable `Test::More` and `Test2` integration for the VS Code Test Explorer panel.

perl-lsp.autoPopulateNewFiles.description: Auto-populate new .pm files with a package declaration and new .t files with Test::More boilerplate.
perl-lsp.autoPopulateNewFiles.markdownDescription: Auto-populate new `.pm` files with a `package` declaration and new `.t` files with `Test::More` boilerplate.

perl-lsp.aiCompletion.enabled.description: Enable AI-powered inline completions. Requires a running language server with AI support.
perl-lsp.aiCompletion.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable AI-powered inline completions. Requires a running language server with AI support.

perl-lsp.aiCompletion.streaming.enabled.description: Enable progressive streaming for AI completions so results appear as they are generated. Requires aiCompletion.enabled to be true.
perl-lsp.aiCompletion.streaming.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable progressive streaming for AI completions so results appear as they are generated. Requires `aiCompletion.enabled` to be true.

enumDescriptions: Track the newest published release.
Stay on the latest stable release line.
Pin the extension to a specific release tag set in versionTag.
perl-lsp.channel.description: Release channel for the perllsp binary: latest for newest release, stable for latest stable line, tag for a pinned version.
perl-lsp.channel.markdownDescription: Release channel for the `perllsp` binary: `latest` for newest release, `stable` for latest stable line, `tag` for a pinned version set in `versionTag`.

perl-lsp.versionTag.description: Specific release tag (for example v0.12.1) to pin when channel is set to tag.
perl-lsp.versionTag.markdownDescription: Specific release tag (for example `v0.12.1`) to pin when `channel` is set to `tag`.

perl-lsp.downloadBaseUrl.description: Internal base URL hosting perllsp archives and SHA256SUMS, bypassing GitHub releases. Leave empty to use GitHub releases.
perl-lsp.downloadBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Internal base URL hosting perllsp archives and SHA256SUMS, bypassing GitHub releases. Example: `https://internal.example.com/perllsp/`. Leave empty to use GitHub releases.

enumDescriptions: Detect Linux libc automatically.
Use the GNU/glibc Linux release asset.
Alias for gnu.
Use the musl Linux release asset.
perl-lsp.linuxLibc.description: Linux libc release asset selection. Keep auto unless you need to force GNU/glibc or musl.
perl-lsp.linuxLibc.markdownDescription: Linux libc release asset selection for managed `perllsp` downloads. Keep `auto` unless you need to force `gnu`/`glibc` or `musl`.

perl-lsp.updateCheckInterval.description: How often (in hours) to check for a new perllsp binary version in the background. Set to 0 to disable automatic update checks.
perl-lsp.updateCheckInterval.markdownDescription: How often (in hours) to check for a new `perllsp` binary version in the background. Set to `0` to disable automatic update checks.

perl-lsp.autoUpdate.description: Automatically download and install a new perllsp binary when an update is available, without prompting. When disabled, a notification is shown instead.
perl-lsp.autoUpdate.markdownDescription: Automatically download and install a new `perllsp` binary when an update is available, without prompting. When disabled, a notification is shown instead.

enumDescriptions: Choose the profile automatically based on the bundled binary.
Lock to the general-availability profile with conservative defaults.
Use the general-availability profile.
Use the production profile (alias for ga).
Use the production profile (alias for ga).
Enable every supported feature, including experimental ones.
perl-lsp.featureProfile.description: Runtime LSP feature profile. auto follows the bundled binary build mode. Use ga or prod for production-safe features only. Use all to enable experimental features.
perl-lsp.featureProfile.markdownDescription: Runtime LSP feature profile. `auto` follows the bundled binary build mode. Use `ga` or `prod` for production-safe features only. Use `all` to enable experimental features.

perl-lsp.disabledFeatures.description: Feature IDs to disable at server startup. Use canonical lsp.* IDs such as lsp.semantic_tokens. Changes take effect after restarting the language server.
perl-lsp.disabledFeatures.markdownDescription: Feature IDs to disable at server startup. Use canonical `lsp.*` IDs such as `lsp.semantic_tokens`. Changes take effect after restarting the language server.

enumDescriptions: Disable protocol tracing.
Log request and response message summaries to the output channel.
Log full verbose protocol details including all message payloads.
perl-lsp.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VS Code and the perl-lsp language server. Set to messages or verbose to capture protocol traffic for debugging.
perl-lsp.trace.server.markdownDescription: Traces the communication between VS Code and the perl-lsp language server. Set to `messages` or `verbose` to capture protocol traffic for debugging.

perl-lsp.mcp.servers.description: Optional MCP stdio servers exposed to VS Code language models. Each entry supports label, command, args, cwd, env, version, and enabled.
perl-lsp.mcp.servers.markdownDescription: Optional MCP stdio servers exposed to VS Code language models. Each entry supports `label`, `command`, `args`, `cwd`, `env`, `version`, and `enabled`.

label.description: Display name shown in MCP server pickers.

command.description: Executable used to start the MCP server.

args.description: Command-line arguments passed to the server.

cwd.description: Optional working directory used when launching the server.

env.description: Optional environment variable overrides (set a key to null to unset).

version.description: Optional MCP server version used by VS Code for refresh prompts.

enabled.description: Set to false to keep a server config without publishing it.
